We welcome most contributions. However, most contributions should fall in our of these three categories:

1. Bug fixes. If you found a new bug, please include a corresponding test.
2. New features.
3. Better performance. Provide benchmark results.

If some static analyzer is giving you are warning, you may issue a pull request: note however that a warning should never be confused as a bug.

Contributions are licensed under the project’s license. Ensure your work complies and does not infringe on third-party rights.

We review manually every change. Make sure that your changes are justified. When in doubt, we prefer not to change existing code.

Review our AI policy is you are using AI: see `AI_USAGE_POLICY.md` are the root of the project.


Before submitting this PR, run `tools/run-clangcldocker.sh` on your changes if you can (requires docker and bash). See the "Contributing" section in the README for details. It is acceptable to skip this formatting.
